sim.collapseTitle: Collapse — keeps the simulation in the charts

marketValue.title: Market value (free)
marketValue.body: Vacant (free) market value of the property. Drives the resale price and the value-unlock at the seller's death.

occupationDiscount.title: Occupation discount (décote)
occupationDiscount.body: Discount reflecting the seller's right to occupy until death. Reduces the market value to the occupied value actually paid. Capped below 100%.

bouquet.title: Bouquet (upfront)
bouquet.body: Upfront lump paid at signing. Plays the purchase-price role: it is the financed amount. Set the down payment equal to bouquet + notary for an all-cash viager.

monthlyAnnuity.title: Monthly annuity (rente)
monthlyAnnuity.body: Rente viagère paid to the seller every month until death. Added to the 'Monthly payment' KPI chip alongside the loan payment.

annuityGrowth.title: Annuity indexation %/yr
annuityGrowth.body: Annual revaluation (indexation) of the rente, usually tied to an index. 0% = fixed annuity.

expectedDuration.title: Expected duration (years)
expectedDuration.body: Seller's expected remaining lifespan. The rente stops at this year and the décote lifts (property value steps up to its full market value). The defining viager bet: die earlier → bargain, later → overpay. A ±5y sensitivity band is shown on the KPIs.

ownerCharges.title: Owner charges (€/yr)
ownerCharges.body: Charges borne by the buyer (bare owner): taxe foncière and major works. Projected across all years.

ownerChargesGrowth.title: Owner charges growth %/yr
ownerChargesGrowth.body: Annual revaluation of the owner charges. Typically 2–3%/yr in France.

inflation.title: Inflation (CPI)
inflation.body: Annual inflation rate. Appreciation and alternative return rates are nominal (e.g. 2% appreciation + 2% inflation = 0% real gain). Used to display real-terms indicators: real IRR (Fisher) and constant-euro wealth in KPIs and charts.

displayReal.title: Nominal / real display
displayReal.body: When on: charts (Comparison and Resale) are shown in constant euros, deflated by inflation. In KPI tables, emphasis moves to the real rows. No effect on the calculation engine (always nominal). No-op when inflation = 0.

regime.title: Rental tax regime
regime.body:
LMNP Real: deductible depreciation (property + works).
Micro-BIC: 50% flat deduction.
Bare rental: actual expenses, no depreciation.

discountRate.title: Discount rate (NPV)
discountRate.body: Rate used to calculate Net Present Value. Represents the opportunity cost of capital.

altReturn.title: ETF / alternative investment return (nominal, after-tax)
altReturn.body: Nominal annual return after tax. Enter after deducting taxation: ~8% gross ETF World → ~5-6% net after 30% flat tax. With 2% inflation, 6% nominal = ~4% real — the real IRR is computed automatically in the KPIs table.

monthlyBudget.title: Monthly housing + investment budget
monthlyBudget.body: Fixed monthly envelope. The surplus not consumed by real costs is automatically invested in ETF.

budgetGrowth.title: Annual budget growth %/yr
budgetGrowth.body: Annual increase of your available budget (salary raises). Grows the ETF surplus each year. 0% = fixed budget.

chargesGrowth.title: Fixed charges annual growth %/yr
chargesGrowth.body: Annual increase rate for property tax, co-ownership charges, insurance and maintenance reserves. Typically 2–3%/yr in France. Management fees (proportional to rent) are not affected.

investSurplus.title: Invest surplus in ETF
investSurplus.body: If enabled: the difference between the monthly budget and real outflows is invested in ETF. The dashed line = pure ETF reference scenario.

etfDownPayment.title: Pure ETF reference contribution
etfDownPayment.body: Starting capital for the pure ETF scenario. No property purchase — contribution + monthly surplus are compounded in ETF.

purchasePrice.title: Net purchase price
purchasePrice.body: Price paid to the seller, excluding notary and agency fees.

notaryFees.title: Notary fees
notaryFees.body: 7-8% for existing properties, 2-3% for new builds. Auto: 8% × purchase price.

renovationCosts.title: Renovation works
renovationCosts.body: Under LMNP Real, depreciable over several years.

agencyFees.title: Buyer's agent fees
agencyFees.body: Agent commission paid by the buyer.

loanFees.title: File fees
loanFees.body: Bank origination (file) fees for obtaining the loan. Included in total acquisition cost and borrowed amount.
loanFees.code: totalCost = purchasePrice + notaryFees + works + agencyFees + loanFees + guaranteeFees + brokerFees

guaranteeFees.title: Loan guarantee fees
guaranteeFees.body: Lender-required guarantee (Crédit Logement-style caution or mortgage/PPD). Included in total cost and borrowed amount. Auto: 1.2% of the estimated loan amount.

brokerFees.title: Broker fees
brokerFees.body: Mortgage broker commission for arranging the loan. Included in total cost and borrowed amount. Auto: 1% of the estimated loan amount.

downPayment.title: Down payment
downPayment.body: Own capital invested. Reduces the loan amount but also the leverage.

interestRate.title: Nominal interest rate
interestRate.body: Nominal loan rate. Auto mode: France average rates (updated 05/2026) — 7y 3.25% · 10y 3.30% · 15y 3.45% · 20y 3.55% · 25y 3.70%.

loanTerm.title: Loan duration
loanTerm.body: Longer = lower monthly payment but higher total cost.

propertyGrowth.title: Property appreciation
propertyGrowth.body: Annual increase assumption. ~2-3%/yr historical average in France.

sellingFees.title: Selling agent fees
sellingFees.body: 3-7% of sale price. Negotiable or avoidable via private sale.

rent.title: Gross monthly rent
rent.body: Excluding recoverable charges. Key driver of profitability.

vacancyRate.title: Vacancy rate
vacancyRate.body: 5-8% in tight markets, 10-15% in rural areas.

propertyTax.title: Property tax
propertyTax.body: Annual local tax. Varies significantly by municipality. Auto: 0.5% × purchase price.

managementFees.title: Property management
managementFees.body: 6-9% incl. tax of collected rents if using an agency.

maintenanceReserve.title: Maintenance reserve
maintenanceReserve.body: Precautionary savings. Rule of thumb: ~1% of value/yr. Auto: 0.5% × purchase price.

rentGrowth.title: Rent increase
rentGrowth.body: Capped by the rent index. 1-4%/yr depending on inflation.

capitalGainsTax.title: Capital gains tax
capitalGainsTax.body: 19% income tax + 17.2% social contributions = 36.2%. Progressive allowances. Primary residence exempt.

personalRent.title: Current rent you pay
personalRent.body: Monthly rent you pay. Rental mode: subtracted from CF. Primary: savings if you buy.
personalRent.code:
Real CF (rental) = Rental CF − Personal rent
Savings (primary) = Personal rent − Monthly payment

personalRentGrowth.title: Market rent revaluation %/yr
personalRentGrowth.body: Annual increase of your rent. ~1-3%/yr indexed.

propertyTaxPrimary.title: Property tax (primary residence)
propertyTaxPrimary.body: Same tax as for rental. No housing tax since 2023 for primary residences. Auto: 0.5% × purchase price.

maintenanceReservePrimary.title: Maintenance reserve (primary)
maintenanceReservePrimary.body: Annual maintenance budget. ~1% of property value. Auto: 0.5% × purchase price.

kpi.totalCost.title: Total acquisition cost
kpi.totalCost.body: Purchase price + notary fees + works + agency fees. This is the base used to compute yields.

kpi.loanAmount.title: Loan amount
kpi.loanAmount.body: Total cost − down payment. Financial leverage amplifies gains and losses.

kpi.monthlyPaymentIns.title: Monthly payment + insurance
kpi.monthlyPaymentIns.body: Fixed annuity + mortgage insurance calculated on the initial capital (fixed for the entire loan term).

kpi.creditCost.title: Total credit cost
kpi.creditCost.body: Sum of all interest payments + all insurance premiums over the full loan term.

kpi.grossYield.title: Gross rental yield
kpi.grossYield.body: Static indicator computed at year 0, before expenses, taxes, and debt service.
kpi.grossYield.code: Gross yield = Rent×12 / Total acquisition cost

kpi.netYield.title: Net rental yield
kpi.netYield.body: Effective rent (after vacancy) − annual expenses, divided by total cost. Excludes debt service and taxes.
kpi.netYield.code: Net yield = (Eff. rent − Expenses) / Total cost

kpi.monthlyCashFlow.title: Real cash-flow / month
kpi.monthlyCashFlow.body: Net monthly flow for year 1: rents collected − all expenses − loan payment − tax − personal rent. Negative = cash effort.

kpi.monthlyEffort.title: Monthly effort
kpi.monthlyEffort.body: Extra monthly cost of the investment vs staying a renter. Positive = you spend more than today. Negative = investment is cheaper.

kpi.breakEven.title: Cash-flow break-even
kpi.breakEven.body: First year where cumulative net cash flows turn positive. Excludes property appreciation.

kpi.irr.title: IRR — Internal Rate of Return
kpi.irr.body: Discount rate that makes the NPV equal to zero. Accounts for sale proceeds, all net flows, and rent saved/paid depending on mode.

kpi.npv.title: NPV — Net Present Value
kpi.npv.body: Sum of future cash flows discounted at the opportunity rate. Positive = investment creates value vs the alternative placement.

kpi.moic.title: MOIC — Multiple on Invested Capital
kpi.moic.body: How many times the down payment has been multiplied at the horizon. Not discounted (unlike NPV).
kpi.moic.code: MOIC = (Net sale + Cumul. flows) / Down payment

kpi.coc.title: Cash-on-cash return (yr 1)
kpi.coc.body: Annual yield of the year-1 net operating cash flow relative to the initial down payment. Simple and immediate: how much cash the property generates per euro invested this year. Does not account for resale value or time value of money. ETF column: a capitalising ETF distributes no cash, so the only real year-1 outflow is the personal rent → −personalRent / etfDownPayment (cash effort, negative).

kpi.resaleBreakEven.title: Resale break-even
kpi.resaleBreakEven.body: First year where reselling no longer makes you lose money, in nominal terms and on the IRR/NPV basis (saved rent for primary / paid rent for rental reintegrated). Accounts for purchase and sale fees. Ignores opportunity cost (an ETF may do better even if you 'don't lose').
kpi.resaleBreakEven.code: first year where netResaleProceeds + Σ(netCashFlow + rent) − downPmt ≥ 0

kpi.cumulativeCashFlow.title: Cumulative cash flow
kpi.cumulativeCashFlow.body: Sum of the annual net cash flows up to the horizon, excluding resale. In rental mode the personal rent paid is counted as an outflow; in primary mode it is charges + loan payments. This is the "flows" component of the Net sale profit (which adds net sale proceeds minus the down payment).

kpi.resaleBalance.title: Net sale profit
kpi.resaleBalance.body: Cash gain if you sell at this horizon: net sale proceeds + cumulative cash flows − initial down payment. Measures what you actually pocketed in cash.

kpi.totalBalance.title: Total sale profit
kpi.totalBalance.body: Overall gain if you sell: net sale proceeds + ETF pocket from reinvested surplus − initial down payment. The most complete metric for comparing two scenarios.

kpi.netWorth.title: Net property wealth
kpi.netWorth.body: Property equity (estimated value − outstanding capital) + cumulative cash flows − initial down payment.

kpi.totalWorth.title: Total wealth
kpi.totalWorth.body: Property equity + ETF pocket built from reinvested monthly surplus. Compared to pure ETF scenario to evaluate the crossover.

kpi.irrReal.title: Real IRR (inflation-adjusted)
kpi.irrReal.body: Nominal IRR adjusted for inflation using the Fisher equation. Measures return in constant euros, above inflation.

kpi.wealthReal.title: Real wealth (constant euros)
kpi.wealthReal.body: Nominal wealth deflated by inflation. Expresses purchasing power in today's euros.

kpi.crossover.title: Crossover vs pure ETF
kpi.crossover.body: First year where total property wealth (equity + ETF from surplus) exceeds the pure ETF. Before this point, the ETF scenario is more profitable.

cf1.title: Cumulative real estate cash-flow — 30 yrs
cf1.desc: Net cumulative flows from the property only. Rental: rents − expenses − mortgage − taxes − personal rent. Primary: rent saved − expenses − mortgage.

pat.title: Total wealth — 30 yrs
pat.desc: Property equity + ETF portfolio built from monthly surplus. Dark dashed = nominal pure ETF. Light dashed = pure ETF in constant euros.
pat.realNote: Nominal euros · light dashed = deflated pure ETF ({{inflation}}%/yr inflation)

cf3.title: Net annual cash-flow (after tax)
cf3.desc: The jump at loan end is normal: monthly payments stop.

vb.title: Property value over time
vb.desc: Estimated nominal market value based on the configured appreciation rate.
vb.realNote: Nominal values · real gain ≈ appreciation rate − {{inflation}}%/yr inflation

resale.title: Net profit by sale year
resale.desc: Net gain if you sell this year. Primary residence exempt from capital gains. Dashed = pure ETF reference.

resaleCash.title: How many years before you stop losing money?
resaleCash.desc: Cash balance if you resell this year, on the same basis as IRR/NPV: net resale + cumulative flows + saved rent (primary) or − paid rent (rental) − down payment. The zero crossing (vertical marker) = minimum holding period to avoid a loss. Nominal values, excluding opportunity cost (vs ETF). cashBalance = netResaleProceeds + Σ(netCashFlow + rent) − down payment

amortization.title: Annual loan breakdown
amortization.desc: At the start most goes to interest, then the capital portion increases progressively.

kpisTable.title: Comparison table — Rental regime:
kpisTable.desc: Total wealth = property equity + ETF from surplus. Pure ETF = contribution + full budget invested without buying.
kpisTable.descReal: Greyed rows = values in constant euros (inflation-adjusted at {{inflation}}%).

auto.tooltipOn: Auto mode active — click to override manually
auto.tooltipOff: Auto mode off — click to enable
auto.zeroGuard: Computed value is 0 — check the purchase price
auto.hint: Fields marked A are auto-calculated. Click A to switch to manual.

monthlyPayment.title: Monthly loan payment
monthlyPayment.body: Constant annuity: a fixed monthly payment that repays the loan over its term. A higher rate or amount raises it; a longer term lowers it (but costs more overall).

amortization.title: Amortization breakdown
amortization.body: Each payment splits between interest (on the outstanding balance) and capital. Early on most of it is interest; the capital share grows year after year.
amortization.code: interest[m] = balance × r/12   ·   capital[m] = payment − interest[m]

insurance.title: Mortgage insurance
insurance.body: Borrower insurance, charged on the initial capital (group-insurance practice), so it stays flat for the whole loan.

creditCost.title: Total cost of credit
creditCost.body: Everything the loan costs beyond the borrowed capital: all interest plus all insurance premiums over the term.

grossYield.title: Gross yield
grossYield.body: Annual rent divided by the total acquisition cost, before any expense or tax. A quick first screen, computed at year 0.

netYield.title: Net yield
netYield.body: Effective rent (after vacancy) minus annual operating expenses, over the total cost. Excludes debt service and tax.
netYield.code: net yield = (rent × (1 − vacancy) × 12 − expenses) / total cost

compounding.title: Compound revaluation
compounding.body: How a value grows when it compounds at a fixed annual rate. The same engine helper powers property appreciation, rent indexation and budget growth.

etfScenario.title: Pure ETF scenario
etfScenario.body: The reference alternative: invest the contribution upfront, then add the monthly surplus (budget − personal rent) every year, all compounding at the ETF return. Net capital applies a 30% flat tax (PFU) on the gain.

rentalTax.title: Rental income tax
rentalTax.body: Tax on rental income depends on the regime. LMNP Real deducts interest and depreciation; Micro-BIC applies a 50% flat allowance; Bare rental deducts actual expenses and interest. Switch the regime to compare.
rentalTax.code: tax = taxable income × (income bracket + social contributions)

allowances.title: Capital-gains allowances
allowances.body: Holding allowances reduce the taxable gain over time. Income tax is fully exempt after 22 years; social contributions after 30 years. Rental only — the primary residence is always exempt.
allowances.code: exempt IR ≥ 22 yrs · exempt PS ≥ 30 yrs

resale.title: Net sale proceeds by year
resale.body: What you actually pocket if you sell in year N: resale price (works included) minus outstanding capital, selling fees and capital-gains tax. The allowances above flatten the tax as the years pass.
resale.code: net = price − balance − fees − gains tax

irrNpvMoic.title: IRR · NPV · MOIC
irrNpvMoic.body: Edit the cash flows (year 0 is your initial outlay). The curve is the NPV at each discount rate — where it crosses zero is the IRR. NPV reads the curve at your discount rate; MOIC is the simple multiple of money out over money in.
